name: Hong Kong SAR
overview: Hong Kong's Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance (PDPO, Cap. 486) dates from 1996 and is built on six Data Protection Principles. A 2021 amendment criminalised doxxing and gave the PCPD strong takedown powers. Broader reform (mandatory breach notification, direct fines) has been under discussion for years; the cross-border transfer provision (s.33) has never been brought into force.

regulator.name: Office of the Privacy Commissioner for Personal Data (PCPD)

legal_instruments.title: Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance (Cap. 486)
legal_instruments.summary: Principles-based law covering collection, accuracy, retention, use, security and access. Direct marketing rules carry criminal penalties; 2021 amendment added anti-doxxing offences and PCPD cessation-notice powers.

legal_instruments.title: Personal Data (Privacy) (Amendment) Ordinance 2021
legal_instruments.summary: Anti-doxxing amendment: criminalises disclosure of personal data without consent with intent or recklessness as to harm, and empowers the Commissioner to investigate, prosecute and issue cessation notices including to overseas platforms.

legal_instruments.title: Guidance on Recommended Model Contractual Clauses for Cross-border Transfer of Personal Data
legal_instruments.summary: Recommended (voluntary) model clauses for cross-border transfers, given that PDPO s.33 restricting transfers has never commenced.

developments.title: Government reiterates PDPO review plans
developments.summary: Officials confirmed continued study of PDPO amendments including mandatory breach notification and administrative fines, but no bill has been introduced.

developments.title: PCPD publishes AI personal data protection framework
developments.summary: The PCPD released a Model Personal Data Protection Framework for organisations procuring and using AI systems.

penalties: No general administrative fines; contravention of an enforcement notice is an offence (up to HKD 50,000 first conviction, higher for repeats). Direct marketing and doxxing offences carry fines up to HKD 1 million and imprisonment.
cross_border: PDPO s.33 (transfer restriction) enacted but never in force; transfers governed by general DPPs. PCPD recommends model contractual clauses voluntarily.
